I'm just an opener. So let me look right into the fucking camera right now until you fly it out That is bullshit I'm telling you if you are in a position right now where you're being led to believe that you're just Opener material you're not worthy of being closer. It's a fucking lie. I promise you With the straight line system. There is absolutely no way under the sun that if you can be a proficient opener That you can't be a proficient closer It's just bullshit now That being said there are many companies where you have to earn your dues You gotta pay your dues right those you have to actually work and be an opener because you're feeding the machine You're feeding a system whereby you're getting started you're being trained you're being mentored, right? And you know you're providing qualified leads through someone who's already been through that process So you're providing a service for the company. That's someone who needs your product. They want your product They value product and they're feeling pain Right now, they're feeling some discomfort about or a lack from not having that need be there's a need They need to be able to sit each they got a scratch something That's making their life feel out of control in some way That's a buyer in heat So they need your product. They want your product and in their own mind They've already made a decision that yes, I gotta do this now So the operative word here what really separates us. This is best buyer Is the word now? They've made a decision that I gotta fill this need now. And then that leaves the other half of the pipeline to be your what's called your look you lose Or tire tickers Look you lose a tire ticker and these are the people Who all I consider To be the most dangerous people of all to your success in the sales field your tire kickers your buyers your look you lose what these people do and Tire kickers comes from the auto industry, right and lookaloo's is from retail They just come in there. You know, they look at everything they want to touch everything, right? They don't buy the reason they're so destructive to you is that they Disguise themselves they disguise themselves as buyers in power Not buyers in heat Just I want you to really under see once you really understand what's going on You'll be able to start making your decisions on the fly of who you should really take down the straight line and who you shouldn't And that gets back to that first thing I said today is about really time management So much of succeeding at sales at the highest level is learned to be an excellent time manager Intradiate how to move and groove and not get bogged down in the bullshit That's so many salesmen do that slow down number of tiles They make and also just take them off course by speaking to unqualified people never buy Right. We have to to really make this your year. You have to master this So I gotta dig deeper into the psychology of what's really going on. So watch Someone who's a buyer in heat they easy to recognize They are so excited. They say things. I'll tell you they'll tell you about their pain as soon as you start asking questions I'll go. Oh my god. Let me tell you my tail. Oh, whoa. It's They're Not their heart, but they wear their pain on their sleeves as the phrase goes that easy to spot, right? The buyers in power are Easy to spot too because they're very attentive They're typically informed. They need your product. They want your product. I probably done some research about it, right? These people here though are the tricksters those like, you know, Loki from Avengers, right? These are the people that disguise themselves as buyers in power So to fully Understand how to really weed them out of your pipeline. You have to know What's really behind them? Why would people do that? Why would someone want to waste your time and Really, what is the the main thing that makes a lookie little retire kicker a tire kicker? So I'm gonna reveal that now. I mean, I've said it in the past here never get laser-guided here So let's dig into this number one The biggest thing the number one thing that separates a lookie-loo From a buyer in power is what they guess anyone? Scratch money 90% of the time seriously 90% of the time they just can't afford it and they want to Experience the thrill of touching something or looking at or getting it and feeling like a big shot from it in a car As if they can afford it Maybe they show it off for something part of them or they just want to you experience it This way take the test drive or whatever it might be try on the clothes the driving force The driving force behind lookie-loos is money. Don't kid yourself. That's what it's about So what I want you to start doing right now is paying a lot closer attention to the financial aspects of your qualification if you find yourself With a lot of people who you seem like they were seem really interested But then just for this where they earned out to be you'll tell because your clothes are be much lower than it should be I just really really interested you run the whole straight line on them right and just time it again And I'll buy it from you that is because of money That's the big one. So you have to start sifting these people out Upfront see what happens is makes one The biggest problem of a lookie-loo is that it's besides the time that you waste with them is That you start thinking that there's something wrong with yourself or your pitch or your methods You say God, I don't get it. I feel I spoke to I pitched 10 people today or I pitched 20 people and only one person bought And there was like seven of them in there that were just like they were they were so interested They seem so great But like I just couldn't get them over the line even I dropped the amount to buy and you're low with your attitude or My with and again, I can't dig into your business without looking at it first hand But my guess is gonna be here is that most of the time that low closing rate was a function of You not spending enough time Weeding out the non buyers from the buyers and you ended up making full-blown Presentation to people who can't fucking buy your product if they're life-dependent because they just can't afford it You get it. They can't afford it and Because of that you start developing all these self-bounds and limiting beliefs you get it now Now we go back to the beginning Why so okay knowing that and by the way, it's not even At this point it can't be like a novel thought like because I've said this before I've told you how toxic these people are Right, so whether you've been through the formal straight line trading out even in these intensives and daily motivations, right? I mentioned this before why would you? knowing this Still allow what's what what's the cause? Why you know why I'll tell you why because you didn't make enough calls in the first place and Because of that Because and that's why it goes back to last week of the the kind of not the all you but the debate I have it's like I don't understand. I always say step one We are not alchemists We don't turn lead into gold. We don't turn one thing into another We're salespeople were sifters We sift through people like the proverbial tin pan or with the scraggly beard and his tin pan And he sits by a stream of fast-running water Why fast-running? Because the faster runs the more water the more molecules that go through his panty sifts and he sifts and he sifts and he knows That if he sits there long enough and waits till enough gallons come through eventually A nugget of gold will drop in his pen But what he's not expecting to happen Because it's physically impossible it can happen is that if you wait till the fucking heavens explode The water is not going to turn into gold They it's not mutable. You don't what is water gold is gold buyers or buyers Non-buyers are not un-non-buys. Most of the people who bought from you will who? That's like dry ice dry ice is amazing thing and Engages in a process called sublimation Sublimation is when you skip something so what when you go from a solid phase to a gas gaseous phase, right solid to gas What's missing water usually you melt something of ice you melt it becomes liquid and then the liquid evaporates it becomes gas, right? That's the normal process Sublimation is like dry ice when it goes from frozen carbon dioxide goes straight from a solid to a gas you skip a step Guess what that process of sublimation? It does not exist in sales You don't have sublimation in other words you have to go buyer in power gets transformed to a buyer in heat and Then he's convinced to buy now That's what's happening and the process the main part of that Occurs while you're prospecting not while you're closing you're always closing clothes, but not in your looping That's later on looping is designed to build certainty and Then in your last step in the process you then reintroduce pain But the point of prospecting is yes, of course to sift. Yeah, you're sifting right, but just as importantly you're beginning or really making a major shot at taking a buyer in power and Turning them into a buyer in heat That's the key and At the same time you're doing that now we get again. We were always doing many things at once, right? Also, you're accomplishing step three Which is you're building massive rapport and the reason that's so important is Because without that rapport if you try to take someone from being a buyer in power to a buyer I think they'll say fuck you who the fuck you know, I hate you stop you You know, you don't create pain in someone Without being in rapport and remain friends with them. There's someone. Okay, let's see soon and On some level I hate using the word pain But it's a great word to use so I use it, but I don't need it in like this disempowering Evil sense. Okay, just let's be clear here. So it's not like pain. You're not trying to cause someone pain It's not about that. And when I talk about taking a person from, you know, who's not feeling pain to make them feel pain I'm not even saying that But I'm it's a shortcut to saying taking someone who's in denial of their pain and Making them realize that they're actually not living the life they want They're not experiencing the full joy the full boat the full the full monty of the whole thing because it's human nature That as we are feeling discomfort for too long We become comfortably numb to it It's a self-protective mechanism It is so what happens is someone that has a lack a certain lack in aspect of their life And you think they should be feeling some discomfort They should be feeling out of control. They should be worried about that I'm doing a training for an insurance company life insurance, right? And I've done many life insurance comes and you find all the time a woman and You find the woman and She should be worried fucking to death that if she dies The kid or the husband dies. They're fucked. They're fucked. So I want the key here. It's a flip. It's a flip of perspective Okay, is that and this is when we start to get to the straight line to really higher levels here of what is really going on when you Prospect yes, of course you're sifting out people you're separating out your non buyers From your buyers and you're building rapport. So they like you because people don't buy really little yeah, blah blah But that's the basic straight line But when we start to dig into the psychology of the straight line of why it works Which allows you to use it so much more effectively it's because of the journey that Every buyer goes through before they say yes and that journey Starts from going from a buyer unless they're already in heat and they're obvious but they're a buyer in power and then you Make them aware That they actually need to be looking at this now and they become a buyer in heat and Then you raise up their levels of certainty so high Above their action threshold and bam People buy which is why the last step of course is what we add on pain again So what's happening to be clear here is that in when we prospect?
